success_surface: Large-scale row filtering and deletion with performance optimization
effective_behaviors: Use vectorized filtering with pandas for bulk row operations on large datasets instead of iterative cell-by-cell modification
Verify file system state before processing (file existence, directory paths)
Inspect workbook structure programmatically before loading data
Identify target columns by position when explicit column names are not guaranteed
Log quantitative change metrics (rows removed, rows remaining) for validation
Ensure output directory exists before attempting to write
Save to new file path to preserve source integrity
evidence: Used pandas.isin() with boolean mask for O(1) row filtering across 13,000+ rows
Loaded workbook with load_workbook() to inspect sheetnames before pandas.read_excel()
Used df.columns[0] to dynamically identify the Group column when name wasn't certain
Printed rows_removed and Remaining rows to provide feedback on operation scope
Saved to /output_path rather than modifying original source file

memory_items.title: Vectorized Operations for Large Datasets
memory_items.lesson: When filtering or deleting many rows from large spreadsheets (>1,000 rows), prefer pandas boolean masking over iterative row deletion.
memory_items.generalization: For bulk row-level operations, use vectorized DataFrame methods like isin(), query(), or boolean indexing instead of looping through rows individually.

memory_items.title: Defensive Column Identification
memory_items.lesson: Column names may vary or be unknown; identify target columns by position as fallback.
memory_items.generalization: When column names are uncertain, use positional references (df.columns[i]) with bounds checking, then optionally validate against expected header patterns.

memory_items.title: Pre-flight System Checks
memory_items.lesson: Validate filesystem state before processing begins.
memory_items.generalization: Always confirm input file exists, output directories can be created, and workbook sheets are accessible before executing modifications.

memory_items.title: Quantified Execution Logging
memory_items.lesson: Report numeric outcomes of operations for verification.
memory_items.generalization: After row/column operations, log counts of affected/remaining entries to enable downstream validation and debugging.

memory_items.title: Directory Preparation Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Target output directories may not exist.
memory_items.generalization: Before writing any output file, ensure parent directories exist using recursive creation with error suppression.

edits.content:
## 6. Performance & Validation Patterns
- **Large Dataset Filtering**: For bulk row operations exceeding 1,000 rows, use pandas vectorized methods (isin(), query()) rather than iterative deletion. Pandas boolean masking processes thousands of rows in sub-second time.
- **Defensive Column Targeting**: When column names are uncertain, identify targets by position (columns[0], columns[n]) with bounds validation before applying filters.
- **Filesystem Pre-Flight**: Always verify input file existence and create output directory hierarchy before execution begins.
- **Change Quantification**: Log exact counts of modified/deleted/remaining rows to enable verification and debug tracking.
- **Non-Destructive Output**: Write all changes to a new filepath unless overwriting is explicitly required.
